channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Southern California to See Cooler Weather with Rain and Thunderstorms This Week
item.description: Southern California residents can expect a shift from the recent record-breaking heat to cooler, wetter weather this week. After an unusually warm March that saw Woodland Hills reach 102°F and Lancast...

item.title: Njombe ex-inmate gets construction tools to start new life after prison
item.description: In Njombe Region, a former prisoner, Mr Rashid Salehe Shemndorwa, has been given construction tools by the Tanzania Prisons Service after completing his 15-year sentence. This initiative is part of th...

item.title: Croma Launches Major Apple Sale With Deep Discounts On iPhone 17
item.description: The NDTV Profit article reportedly covered a major promotion involving the iPhone 17 at Croma retail stores in India. According to the headline, the smartphone's price was heavily discounted, dropping...

item.title: SAPS captain arrested for alleged misappropriation of presidential protection funds
item.description: A 53-year-old police captain stationed at Sunnyside police station has been arrested for allegedly misappropriating millions from the South African Police Service's presidential protection unit. Accor...

item.title: Ernestilla Bahati: Guiding Investors Through Tanzania’s Changing Corporate Environment
item.description: For more than ten years, Ms Ernestilla Bahati has built her reputation as one of Tanzania’s leading legal minds in investment and corporate advisory. As the founder and managing partner of Ernestilla,...

item.title: PhilHealth spends P16.4 billion for YAKAP program to support Filipinos' healthcare
item.description: The Philippine Health Insurance Corp. (PhilHealth) has disbursed a total of P16.4 billion in payments for the Yaman ng Kalusugan program (YAKAP), which aims to provide comprehensive outpatient service...

item.title: Arizona hiker hospitalized after more than 100 bee stings during mountain rescue
item.description: An Arizona hiker is in critical condition after being attacked by a swarm of bees while hiking on Lookout Mountain last Saturday morning. According to the Phoenix Fire Department, rescue teams from Ph...

item.title: First-Hand Account of the Historic Artemis II Launch and Crew Journey
item.description: The article narrates the personal experience of a correspondent who attended the incredible lift-off of Artemis II, NASA’s landmark mission aiming to send astronauts around the Moon. The writer descri...

item.title: Exploring Australia’s Oldest Playable Musical Instruments: From Yidaki to Historic European Bass
item.description: Australia’s musical history includes instruments that predate European colonisation, with some still playable today. Among the oldest is a yidaki, sacred to the Yolŋgu people, restored for an exhibiti...

item.title: Ex-soldier alleges army doesn’t provide uniforms and protective gear
item.description: One former Nigerian Army corporal, Rotimi Olamilekan, wey dem dey call Soja Boi online, don raise serious concerns about the living conditions of soldiers. According to him, the army no dey provide ba...

item.title: AustralianSuper reshuffles portfolio, reduces stake in CSL and James Hardie
item.description: AustralianSuper, Australia's largest superannuation fund, has significantly reduced its holdings in key Australian stocks, including CSL and James Hardie, as part of a strategic shift in its investmen...

item.title: Wells Fargo cuts energy sector rating amid limited long-term oil price outlook
item.description: The Wells Fargo Investment Institute (WFII) has downgraded its rating for the S&P 500 energy sector from 'neutral' to 'unfavourable,' citing limited prospects for a sustained oil price premium despite...

item.title: Rajya Sabha Chairman Rejects Impeachment Motion Against Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar
item.description: The Rajya Sabha Chairman CP Radhakrishnan has rejected the impeachment motion moved against Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar, ending a major opposition effort to seek his removal. The motion ...

item.title: Proposed ceasefire and peace plan sent to US and Iran amid Hormuz tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran have both received a proposal aiming to stop ongoing hostilities and open a path toward long-term peace. This development comes shortly after President Donald Trump threaten...

item.title: Israeli Prime Minister Replaces Chief of Staff Over Racist Comments and Internal Party Trouble
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu don remove him acting chief of staff, Ziv Agmon, after some leaked audio come out wey show say the man make racist comments. According to Netanyahu, Agmon go ...

item.title: Construction of Border Wall on Mount Cristo Rey Begins, Sparking Local Controversy
item.description: Blasting activities have begun on the south side of Mount Cristo Rey, a cherished landmark on the border of New Mexico and Texas, to make way for the construction of a border wall. This move follows a...

item.title: US Supreme Court paves the way for dismissal of Steve Bannon's contempt conviction
item.description: The US Supreme Court has ruled that the Justice Department can proceed with dismissing the contempt of Congress case against Steve Bannon, an ally of former President Donald Trump. Bannon was convicte...

item.title: Masked Men Breach Delhi Assembly Security and Place Bouquet in Speaker's Car
item.description: On April 6, 2026, Delhi police detained three suspects after a security breach at the Delhi Assembly. The incident took place when a vehicle registered in Uttar Pradesh broke through Gate No. 2 of the...

item.title: Savannah Guthrie Returns to 'Today' Amid Ongoing Search for Her Missing Mother
item.description: US television anchor Savannah Guthrie made her emotional return to NBC's Today show on Monday, more than two months after her mother, Nancy Guthrie, went missing from her Arizona home. Opening the sho...

item.title: Australian Tourists Adjust Travel Plans Despite Middle East Conflict
item.description: Despite the ongoing conflict in Iran, Australian tourists continue to head to Europe, adapting their travel plans to avoid cancellations and minimise losses. Travel agents across the continent report ...
